data.description: • JANE STREET ON INDIA'S SEBI ORDER: JANE STREET DISPUTES THE FINDINGS OF THE SEBI INTERIM ORDER AND WILL FURTHER ENGAGE WITH THE REGULATOR

data.title: Market Chatter: Xpeng Launches G7 SUV
data.description:
Xpeng launched its G7 SUV in Beijing on Thursday at a starting price of 195,800 yuan, the South China Morning Post reported on the same day.

The electric car maker is looking to compete against Tesla's Model Y and Xiaomi's recently announced YU7, with the new model reportedly fitted with the company's in-house Turing AI chip which is capable of hosting a large language model, the report said.

The G7 is also reportedly capable of Level 3 self-driving, according to the report.

The model's top-of-the line variant costs 225,800 yuan, the SCMP reported.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: BetterAlt Partners with Sprouts Farmers Market to Expand Retail Presence Nationwide
data.description:
DOVER, Del., July 03, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — BetterAlt, a rising leader in Ayurvedic and science-backed wellness, has officially partnered with Sprouts Farmers Market to make its best-selling products available in 425 locations across the United States. The collaboration significantly expands BetterAlt’s reach in physical retail, offering Sprouts customers convenient access to a curated line of natural supplements formulated for energy, performance, and holistic wellness.

The initial rollout includes BetterAlt’s most in-demand products:

  • Himalayan Shilajit Resin
  • Himalayan Shilajit Honey Sticks (Classic and Caramel)
  • Himalayan Shilajit Gummies (Mango)
  • Himalayan Shilajit Capsules
  • Tongkat Ali Cordyceps Capsules
  • Shilajit Sea Moss Capsules

“Partnering with Sprouts is an exciting milestone for us,” said Akash Dhoot, cofounder at BetterAlt. “They’re known for putting quality and transparency first - values we care deeply about. Together, we’re making it easier for people to bring time-tested, nature-based wellness into their daily lives.”

Founded on the principles of Ayurveda and enhanced by modern science, BetterAlt’s products are third-party lab tested at ISO-certified laboratories and formulated to support energy, stamina, cognitive performance, and daily vitality. The company has quickly gained momentum on digital platforms, including Amazon and TikTok, selling over 15 million servings of Himalayan Shilajit and 1.5 million customers within its first year of operations.

The launch into Sprouts stores is part of BetterAlt’s broader growth strategy to increase accessibility in high-traffic wellness retail channels while continuing to innovate in functional nutrition. With a focus on consumer trust and quality assurance, the brand is helping reshape how ancient remedies are adopted in modern lifestyles.

About BetterAlt

BetterAlt is a global health and wellness company blending traditional Ayurvedic wisdom with modern science to develop natural supplements for today's fast-paced world. Best known for its Himalayan Shilajit-based products, BetterAlt offers functional wellness in various consumer-friendly formats—from resins and capsules to gummies and honey sticks. The company has experienced rapid growth, achieving $100 million in annual revenue and 5x the growth rate. Recognized among the top 3 health brands on TikTok Shop, BetterAlt’s products consistently rank #1 on Amazon in their categories and are also available through major retailers like GNC. Headquartered in the United States, the company currently serves markets across North America, Asia, and Europe.

Contact Information:

Contact Person's Name: Akash Dhoot

Organization / Company: BetterAlt

Company website: thebetteralt.com

Contact Email Address: press@thebetteralt.com

City, State / Province, Country, Zip Code: Dover, Delaware, 19904

A photo accompanying this announcement is available at https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/cd3d37a7-ebf7-48fd-b0f4-d1932aa8fa6d

BetterAlt Partners with Sprouts Farmers Market to Expand Retail Presence Nationwide

data.title: South Korea's Finance Ministry to Boost Report Writing Efficiency with New AI Tools
data.description:
South Korea's Ministry of Strategy and Finance is expanding its use of AI tools to improve work efficiency, particularly in the area of report writing, according to a Friday release by the ministry.

After launching an internal "AI Hub" in February that gives employees access to ChatGPT and Perplexity, with over 4,000 monthly uses, the ministry said it will now roll out a Korean report writing support program.

The tool offers standardized templates for key report types, including press releases and National Assembly materials, plus built-in graph, table, and formatting aids, the release said.

A dedicated data and generative AI platform is also planned for 2026, it said.

data.title: British Business - July 4
data.description:
The following are the top stories on the business pages of British newspapers. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

The Times

- Specialised life insurance group Athora has agreed to buy UK insurer Pension Insurance Corporation (PIC), for about 5.7 billion pounds ($7.78 billion) both companies said on Thursday.

- Global commodities trade Glencore was only informed of the financial difficulties at Britain's Prax Lindsey oil refinery three days before it collapsed despite ministers being aware of the difficulties for several months.

The Guardian

- The EU and US are closing in on a high-level "framework" trade deal that would avert 50% tariffs on all exports from the bloc next Wednesday, Donald Trump's self-imposed deadline, with diplomats and officials saying the EU was willing to accept Trump's 10% blanket tariffs.

- Richard Branson's hopes of returning Virgin trains to the west coast mainline have been quelled after the UK's rail regulator, the Office of Rail and Road rebuffed three applications for new additional train services on Britain's busiest line.

The Telegraph

- Britain risks running out of space for offshore wind farms, Dan McGrail, interim chief executive of GB Energy said, adding that UK waters are becoming too crowded for traditional turbines built in shallower seas.

- Sonja Laud, the chief investment officer at Legal & General said that the UK government's decision to abandon key benefit reforms and reverse course on winter fuel payments had destroyed faith in the Government's economic plans.

Sky News

- Ryanair , Europe's largest airline by passenger numbers, said it had axed 170 services amid a plea by French authorities for airlines to reduce flights at Paris airports by 40% on Friday.

($1 = 0.7325 pounds)

data.title: Aussie tech stocks climb, track Nasdaq's record-high session
data.description:
** Australian technology stocks rise 1.5% to 2,920.6 points, on track for their best day since June 10, if gains hold

** The sub-index tracks rally in Wall Street's tech-heavy Nasdaq , which closed at a record high overnight

** Logistics software firm WiseTech Global rises 1.6% to A$112.65, its highest point since February 19

** Shares of accounting software maker Xero gain 1.6%

** ASX-listed shares of Life360 feature among top 10 gainers in the benchmark index with a 2.7% jump

** YTD, AXIJ sub-index up nearly 5% vs AXJO up 5.5%

data.title: China Vanke Secures Up to 6.25 Billion-Yuan Loan from Shareholder
data.description:
China Vanke secured an up to 6.25 billion-yuan loan from its largest shareholder, Shenzhen Metro Group, to help repay bonds.

The three-year loan has an interest rate of 66 basis points below the one-year loan prime rate (currently 2.34%), according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

The residential development and property services company will also extend an 890 million-yuan loan to Dec. 31 and pledge shares of subsidiary Onewo as collateral for another 1.55 billion-yuan loan.

data.title: Yomiuri: Japan Posts 2.264 Trillion Yen General Account Surplus for FY24
data.description:
By Mayumi Terashima

Yomiuri Shimbun Staff Writer

Japan's current account surplus for fiscal 2024 is 2.264 trillion yen, the Finance Ministry announced Wednesday, marking the third largest surplus in history.

Factors cited by the ministry for the surplus include widespread wage increases and rise in tax revenue driven by strong corporate performance.

Tax revenue reached a record high of 75.232 trillion yen, up 1.797 trillion yen from the November 2024 estimate. Non-tax revenue, including payments from the Bank of Japan, rose by 1.605 trillion yen.

Unused funds allocated in the budget for expenditures totaled 4.31 trillion yen.

Because of the increase in revenue and unused funds, the government will cancel the issuance of 5 trillion yen in deficit bonds planned for the fiscal 2024 budget and increase allocations, including local government transfers, by 449 billion yen.

In accordance with the Public Finance Law, half of the surplus funds will be allocated to repaying government bonds, and the government plans to allocate the remaining 1.1 trillion yen to strengthening defense capabilities.

----

This article is from The Yomiuri Shimbun. Neither Dow Jones Newswires, MarketWatch, Barron's nor The Wall Street Journal were involved in the creation of this content.

YDN-m0000126146-1

data.title: Malaysia's Glove Sector May See Slow Recovery in 2H — Market Talk
data.description: Malaysia's glove sector may see a rebound in 2H albeit at a slower pace, as low inventories prompt order replenishment and global buyers shift from Chinese producers due to rising tariffs, says Kenanga IB analyst Raymond Choo Ping Khoon in a note. Potential policy changes in the EU and the U.S. on Chinese gloves could drive up demand, he says. Despite near-term earnings weakness, the sector's long-term prospects may remain positive, supported by structural demand growth and ongoing supply rationalization, he adds. Kenanga maintains an overweight call on the sector, favoring Hartalega and Kossan Rubber Industries for exposure. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Copper set for weekly gains; potential US tariffs on metal eyed
data.description:
Copper prices on the London Metal Exchange and Shanghai Futures Exchange were set for a second straight weekly gain on Friday, despite minor fluctuations, while traders looked out for potential U.S. tariffs on copper imports.

Three-month copper on the LME added 0.06% to $9,960 per metric ton by 0106 GMT, rising 0.85% so far this week, while the most-traded copper contract on the SHFE eased 0.31% to 80,510 yuan ($11,231.24), gaining 1.02% this week.

"The dollar has strengthened a bit as (the) United States may not cut interest rates soon with better-than-expected payrolls and unemployment data, the 'big beautiful bill' has been passed and the focus of the copper market has been back to the possible import tariffs by the U.S.," a Beijing-based metals analyst with a futures company said.

Two analysts from China dismissed the significance of the recent rises in total copper stocks (MCUSTX-TOTAL) in LME-registered warehouses. The volume grew by 3,700 tons or 4.1% for three consecutive days to July 3, after declining gradually from mid-April.

"Copper will be shipped to the U.S. from elsewhere so long as the U.S. tariff is not a done deal," a Shanghai-based metals analyst with a futures company said.

The COMEX copper premium over LME prices stood at around $1,300 a ton on Thursday, while canceled warrants, or metal earmarked to leave LME warehouses, totalled 31,900 tons.

LME nickel slipped 0.33% to $15,400 a ton and zinc lost 0.31% to $2,742.

SHFE nickel gained 0.75% to 122,400 yuan a ton, lead rose 0.12% to 17,280 yuan and tin added 0.08% to 269,220 yuan. Aluminium shed 0.12% to 20,660 yuan.

For the top stories in metals and other news, click

or

0830 UK S&P GLOBAL PMI: MSC COMPOSITE - OUTPUT June

($1 = 7.1684 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Australian Household Spending Jumps Suddenly on Rate-Cuts Boost
data.description:
By James Glynn

SYDNEY--Australian consumer demand showed signs of life in May as the impact of interest-rate cuts over recent months drove a jump in spending.

Household spending rose 0.9% in May, the Australian Bureau of Statistics said Friday. The result followed a flat result in April and a 0.1% fall in March.

The rise in May was driven by spending on discretionary goods which climbed 1.1% over the month. Spending on clothing, footwear, dining out and new vehicle led the rise, the data showed.

The strong data comes just ahead of a policy meeting at the Reserve Bank of Australia on Monday and Tuesday, with money market traders betting the central bank is virtually assured of lowering the official cash rate.

With consumer demand not as flat as expected, the RBA could find a case to wait a few weeks in order to absorb second quarter inflation data at the end of this month, putting off a further cut in interest rates until August.

"This series is volatile and can be revised, but this was the largest monthly gain since October," said Kieran Davies, chief macro strategist at Coolabah Capital.

Seven of the nine spending categories measured in the data rose in May, but there was weakness in sales of alcoholic beverages, tobacco, and food, the data showed.

Household spending was 4.2% higher than the same time last year, with spending on services up 7.5% in that time, the ABS said

Write to James Glynn at james.glynn@wsj.com

data.title: U.S. Tariff Deal Strengthens Vietnam's Outlook — Market Talk
data.description: Vietnam's trade deal with the U.S. brightens its outlook, say analysts at BMI, a unit of Fitch Solutions. They reckon Vietnam managed to clinch a deal faster than others because it sought relatively lower tariffs rather than insisting on zero, and offered a major market-opening package. Despite the agreed-upon 20% levy, Vietnam's exports are set to keep growing steadily, BMI says. Vietnam's cost competitiveness will likely keep production from relocating to other economies, and BMI expects foreign direct investment to continue flowing. The certainty the deal provides will assure U.S. investors that Vietnam can still be part of their supply chains, and Chinese firms will still be incentivized to move production to Vietnam. BMI now thinks Vietnam's 2025 GDP growth could top its forecast of 6.4%. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

data.title: Australia's Household Spending Rises 0.9% in May
data.description:
Australia's seasonally adjusted household spending rose 0.9% to AU$76.45 billion in May after a flat reading in April, the Australian Bureau of Statistics reported on Friday.

Seven of the nine spending categories lifted in May. Clothing and footwear, transport, and miscellaneous goods and services led the increase, with spending up by 3.7%, 1.7%, and 1.3%, respectively. Alcoholic beverages and tobacco, and food were the only categories to decline by 1.4% and 0.1%, respectively.

"Discretionary spending rose 1.1%, as households spent more on clothing and footwear, new vehicles, and dining out," said Robert Ewing, the bureau's head of business statistics.

Non-discretionary spending increased by 0.5%, marking its fifth consecutive month of growth, Ewing added.

Compared with the year-ago period, household spending rose 4.2% in May.

data.title: Shares of Finbar Rise After Unit Signs AU$13.7 Million Land Sale Deal
data.description:
Shares of Finbar Group (ASX: FRI) edged up about 1% in early Friday trade after the company said in a late Thursday Australian bourse filing that its unit entered into a contract to sell a development site at Rivervale for AU$13.7 million to Together Housing, a registered charity and community housing provider.

The deal is conditional upon the purchaser's satisfaction of feasibility benchmarks, and is expected to boost the company's fiscal 2026 earnings, the filing said.

data.title: Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacifico Reports a Passenger Traffic Increase in June 2025 of 0.6% Compared to 2024
data.description:
GUADALAJARA, Mexico, July 03, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacífico, S.A.B. de C.V., (NYSE: PAC; BMV: GAP) (“the Company” or “GAP”) announces preliminary terminal passenger traffic figures for June 2025, compared with June 2024.

During this period, the total number of terminal passengers at GAP’s 12 Mexican airports increased by 0.7%, compared to June 2024. Los Cabos airport presented an increase in passenger traffic of 1.7%, while Tijuana, Guadalajara, and Puerto Vallarta airports decreased 2.3%, 1.1%, and 0.1% respectively, compared to June 2024. On the other hand, Montego Bay presented a decrease in passenger traffic of 1.9%, compared to June 2024.

Highlights for the month:

  • Seats and load factors: The seats available during June 2025 increased by 2.1%, compared to June 2024. The load factors for the month went from 83.4% in June 2024 to 82.2% in June 2025.

Company Description

Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacífico, S.A.B. de C.V. (GAP) operates 12 airports throughout Mexico’s Pacific region, including the major cities of Guadalajara and Tijuana, the four tourist destinations of Puerto Vallarta, Los Cabos, La Paz and Manzanillo, and six other mid-sized cities: Hermosillo, Guanajuato, Morelia, Aguascalientes, Mexicali, and Los Mochis. In February 2006, GAP’s shares were listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol “PAC” and on the Mexican Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol “GAP”. In April 2015, GAP acquired 100% of Desarrollo de Concessioner Aeroportuarias, S.L., which owns a majority stake in MBJ Airports Limited, a company operating Sangster International Airport in Montego Bay, Jamaica. In October 2018, GAP entered into a concession agreement for the Norman Manley International Airport operation in Kingston, Jamaica, and took control of the operation in October 2019.

data.title: Tencent Music Entertainment Target Price Raised to $23.00 From $17.00 by Citi >TME
Shivam Autotech Board OKs INR200 Million Debenture Allotment
data.description:
Shivam Autotech received board approval on Thursday to raise 200 million Indian rupees, through the allotment of non-convertible debentures on a private placement basis, according to a Friday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The auto component manufacturer's board approved the allotment of 2,000 secured unlisted, unrated redeemable non-convertible debentures of a face value of 100,000 rupees each.

The debentures carry a coupon rate of 12.85% per annum with a tenure of up to 48 months.

data.title: Tencent Music's Ximalaya Acquisition Seen to Support Subscription Initiative — Market Talk
data.description: Tencent Music Entertainment's acquisition of podcast platform Ximalaya fits well with its latest subscription initiative, Citi analysts say in a research note. TME has been working on converting subscribers to premium subscribers, which proves to be a more sustainable and resilient approach to ensure steady music subscription revenue growth. The addition of Ximalaya's long-form content to TME platform could enhance monetization potential through subscriber conversion and ads. While maintaining a buy rating on TME, Citi raises its target price to US$23.00 from US$17.00, noting the stable subscription model with improving margins profile and future upside of artists/fan-based entertainment ecosystem fits well with the growing service consumption among Chinese young consumers. Shares last ended at US$19.46. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0213 GMT - Tencent Music Entertainment's acquisition of podcast platform Ximalaya fits well with its latest subscription initiative, Citi analysts say in a research note. TME has been working on converting subscribers to premium subscribers, which proves to be a more sustainable and resilient approach to ensure steady music subscription revenue growth. The addition of Ximalaya's long-form content to TME platform could enhance monetization potential through subscriber conversion and ads. While maintaining a buy rating on TME, Citi raises its target price to US$23.00 from US$17.00, noting the stable subscription model with improving margins profile and future upside of artists/fan-based entertainment ecosystem fits well with the growing service consumption among Chinese young consumers. Shares last ended at US$19.46. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0205 GMT - Bangkok Chain Hospital's EPS growth outlook of 17% for 2025 and 10% for 2026 is likely the strongest in the sector, Thanachart Securities' Siriporn Arunothai says in a research report. This outlook is backed by rising revenue from both Thai and international cash patients, increased income from a social security scheme and improving EBIT margins. Also, the hospital is likely seeing minor impact from the Thailand-Cambodia border dispute, while its stock is trading at an attractive 1.4x 2025 price/earnings-to-growth ratio compared with the sector average of 5.0x, the analyst adds. The brokerage has a buy rating and a target price of THB19.00 on the shares, which last closed at THB13.30. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0158 GMT - The Malaysian consumer sector's 3Q earnings are likely to be weighed by softer spending due to rising living costs and domestic policy uncertainties, says Affin Hwang IB. Consumer spending may slow amid the expansion of service tax and the impending RON95 fuel subsidy changes, says analyst Peggie Wong Pei Chi in a note. However, sentiment could rebound in 4Q, supported by festive demand and improved policy clarity. Affin Hwang maintains a neutral outlook on Malaysia's consumer sector and prefers AEON Co. (M) and Heineken Malaysia for their resilience. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0144 GMT - South Korean auto stocks appear to be trading at a discount with their fundamentals and opportunities in physical artificial intelligence seemingly overlooked, Daiwa Capital analysts Henny Jung and Yoonki Bae say in a note. The new Seoul government's pledge to invest 100 trillion won, equivalent to $73.34 billion, in AI and expand related infrastructure will likely accelerate the commercialization of autonomous driving and humanoid robots, where Hyundai Motor Group is already active through its affiliates, the analysts say. They choose the group's flagship carmaker Hyundai Motor and its auto-component-making affiliate Hyundai Mobis as their top picks in the local auto sector. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0137 GMT - Malaysia's oil-and-gas sector is expected to see steady upstream activity and strong midstream gas demand, MIDF says in a note. Liquefied natural gas infrastructure growth, rising power needs from data centers, and regional energy transition efforts should support midstream resilience, it says. However, weak petrochemical margins and potential oil oversupply remain key risks, it adds. While geopolitical tensions may lift short-term prices, MIDF maintains a neutral rating on the sector. It favors MISC for its diversified earnings and exposure to long-haul shipping and floating production storage and offloading vessel projects.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0120 GMT - Pro Medicus looks too pricey for Morgans analyst Iain Wilkie to chase hard after the stock, even with the medical imaging-tech provider's strong start to the fiscal year. Wilkie maintains a trim recommendation on the Australia-listed stock. Pro Medicus's two new contracts are viewed positively, but Wilkie says in a note that it is hard to see how the company can increase its growth rate of recent years. The number of large contract opportunities in the pipeline is diminishing, he adds. Morgans raises its target price 12% to A$280.00. Shares are up 0.1% at A$307.58. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0118 GMT - Malaysian banks may perform well ahead of an anticipated market recovery in late 2H, Hong Leong IB analyst Eddy Do says in a note. He sees 2H as a period of stark contrast for the domestic equities market, with 3Q expected to remain volatile before conditions stabilize in 4Q. However, the market appears increasingly desensitized to trade-related rhetoric, suggesting peak uncertainty may have passed. The banking sector's defensive earnings resilience, attractive dividend yields, and undemanding valuations could provide downside support amid the market volatility, he adds. Hong Leong maintains an overweight rating on Malaysia's banking sector, and pegs CIMB Group, AMMB Holdings and RHB Bank as top picks. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0107 GMT - Oiltek International's share price may break above a wedge pattern, in which it has been consolidating since mid-May, Phillip Securities Research's Zane Aw says in commentary. The moving average convergence divergence indicator is holding above the zero line and could form a bullish crossover soon, which would underpin the bullish view, the analyst says. If the share price breaks above the wedge pattern on the technical chart, it could rise further and reach S$0.710, a swing high resistance level formed at the start of May, the analyst adds. Shares are unchanged at S$0.590. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0102 GMT - Australian lenders Pepper Money and Australian Finance Group both lose their bull at Citi despite their considerable earnings growth profiles over the coming years. Analyst Thomas Strong lowers his rating on both stocks to neutral from buy, telling clients that they are trading close to cyclically high earnings multiples. He sees the prospect of shares growing into their multiples while investors become more comfortable with earnings profiles and competitive dynamics. Short-term capital-management catalysts appear to be played out, he adds. Citi raises its target price on Pepper by 21% to A$1.75. The stock is down 0.4% at A$1.8075. Citi raises its target price on AFG by 14% to A$2.10. The stock is down 2.3% at A$2.11. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0043 GMT - Domino's Pizza Enterprises' strategy for achieving its 3% same-store sales-growth target still isn't clear to Morgan Stanley analysts. They tell clients in a note that the Australian fast-food franchiser has flagged the importance of hitting its sales-growth target without clearly articulating how to get there. The MS analysts observe that the long-standing competitive advantage from the company's proprietary technology has been eroded. There is an opportunity to reinvest planned tech savings into improving franchisee profitability but the timing and quantum of any efforts are unknown, they add. MS has a last-published equal-weight rating and A$24.00 target price on the stock, which is up 0.2% at A$17.36. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0013 GMT - Japanese stocks are higher in early trade after solid U.S. jobs data boost optimism over the U.S. economic outlook. Bank and electronics stocks are leading the gains. Mizuho Financial Group is up 1.7% and Advantest is 1.8% higher. USD/JPY is at 144.70, up from 143.89 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are closely watching any developments related to U.S. tariffs as well as domestic policymaking ahead of the upper-house election later this month. The Nikkei Stock Average is up 0.3% at 39920.82. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

2346 GMT - Hub24's bull at Bell Potter reckons that consensus forecasts for the Australian wealth platform haven't fully factored in share markets' recent recovery. Analyst Hayden Nicholson points out in a note to clients that all major indexes gained over the June quarter. Reeling off a list of significant moves by global indexes, Nicholson observes with hindsight that the S&P/ASX 200's performance is a strong predictor of Hub24's funds under management. The Australian benchmark index gained 9.5% over the three months through June, he adds. Nicholson raises his fiscal 4Q funds under management assumption by 3%. Bell Potter raises its target price 33% to A$100.00 and keeps a buy rating on the stock. Shares are at A$89.21 ahead of the open. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Mr. D.I.Y's New Store Format Likely Has Limited Earnings Impact — Market Talk
data.description: Mr. D.I.Y. Group (M) refreshing its MR.TOY brand with a more experiential store format is aimed at boosting engagement and basket sizes, Kenanga IB analyst Thin Yun Jing says in a note. MR.TOY is the Malaysian home-improvement retailer's toy store chain. While earnings impact remains limited for now, the new concept could serve as a blueprint to scale and widen appeal, she says. Kenanga raises the stock's target price to MYR2.04 from MYR1.80 as its rolls forward valuation base year, and maintains an outperform rating on the stock. Shares are unchanged at MYR1.66. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Australian Dollar Holds Near 8-Month High
data.description:
The Australian dollar held around $0.657 on Friday, hovering near eight-month highs, despite widespread expectations that the RBA will implement its third interest rate cut of the year at its July 8 monetary policy meeting.

The central bank is expected to lower rates by another 25 basis points amid cooling inflation and a weakening economic outlook.

Inflation stood at 2.4% in Q1 2025, remaining at its lowest level since early 2021 and still within the RBA’s 2–3% target range.

Meanwhile, GDP grew just 0.2% in Q1, down from 0.6% in Q4 2024.

Economists and markets initially expected three RBA rate cuts this year but raised their forecasts to four in May, and now anticipate up to five, driven by faster-than-expected disinflation and signs of economic weakness.

Still, despite growing expectations of deeper rate reductions, the Australian dollar has risen more than 6% year-to-date, buoyed by broad-based weakness in the US dollar.

data.title: Bangkok Chain Hospital's EPS Growth Outlook Likely Strongest in Sector — Market Talk
data.description: Bangkok Chain Hospital's EPS growth outlook of 17% for 2025 and 10% for 2026 is likely the strongest in the sector, Thanachart Securities' Siriporn Arunothai says in a research report. This outlook is backed by rising revenue from both Thai and international cash patients, increased income from a social security scheme and improving EBIT margins. Also, the hospital is likely seeing minor impact from the Thailand-Cambodia border dispute, while its stock is trading at an attractive 1.4x 2025 price/earnings-to-growth ratio compared with the sector average of 5.0x, the analyst adds. The brokerage has a buy rating and a target price of THB19.00 on the shares, which last closed at THB13.30. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Bitcoin Price Coiling Up — Is a Surge Past $110K on Deck?
data.description:
Bitcoin price started a fresh increase above the $107,500 zone. BTC is now consolidating and might struggle to continue higher above the $110,000 resistance.

  • Bitcoin started a fresh increase above the $108,500 zone.
  • The price is trading above $108,000 and the 100 hourly Simple moving average.
  • There is a short-term rising channel forming with support at $109,500 on the hourly chart of the BTC/USD pair (data feed from Kraken).
  • The pair could start a fresh increase if it stays above the $107,850 zone.

Bitcoin Price Consolidates Gains

Bitcoin price started a fresh increase after it settled above the $105,500 resistance. BTC cleared many hurdles near $107,000 to start a decent increase.

The bulls pushed the price in a positive zone above the $108,000 level. The price even spiked above the $110,000 level before the bears appeared. A high was formed near $110,578 and the price is now consolidating gains near the 23.6% Fib retracement level of the upward move from the $105,116 swing low to the $110,578 high.

Bitcoin is now trading above $108,000 and the 100 hourly Simple moving average. Besides, there is a short-term rising channel forming with support at $109,500 on the hourly chart of the BTC/USD pair.

On the upside, immediate resistance is near the $110,000 level. The first key resistance is near the $110,500 level. A close above the $110,500 resistance might send the price further higher. In the stated case, the price could rise and test the $112,000 resistance level. Any more gains might send the price toward the $113,200 level.

Downside Correction In BTC?

If Bitcoin fails to rise above the $110,000 resistance zone, it could start another decline. Immediate support is near the $109,500 level and the channel’s trend line. The first major support is near the $108,800 level.

The next support is now near the $107,850 zone and the 50% Fib retracement level of the upward move from the $105,116 swing low to the $110,578 high. Any more losses might send the price toward the $106,400 support in the near term. The main support sits at $105,000, below which BTC might continue to move down.

Technical indicators:

Hourly MACD – The MACD is now losing pace in the bullish zone.

Hourly RSI (Relative Strength Index) – The RSI for BTC/USD is now above the 50 level.

Major Support Levels – $108,800, followed by $107,850.

Major Resistance Levels – $110,000 and $110,500.

data.title: FTSE Straits Times Index Hits Record Intraday High
Mr. D.I.Y. Group (M) Target Price Raised to MYR2.04 From MYR1.80 by Kenanga IB >5296.KU
FTSE Straits Times Index Last at 4011.44 After Marking New High of 4024.33 Earlier
Qatar Non-Oil Private Sector Growth at 3-Month High
data.description:
Qatar’s S&P Global Purchasing Managers’ Index (PMI) rose to 52.0 in June 2025, up from 50.8 in May, signaling the fastest growth in the non-energy private sector since March.

The latest reading matches the highest level recorded so far in 2025, although it remains slightly below the long-run survey average of 52.2.

Output continued to expand, while employment rose, with the pace of job creation reaching the joint-third highest on record.

Employment growth was observed across all sectors, led by manufacturing.

The rise in staffing levels was supported by a further increase in outstanding business, the sharpest since last October.

Wage growth was also notable, recording the second-highest increase on record, just behind January.

On the price front, selling prices declined for the eleventh consecutive month, with the rate of deflation the fastest since last November.

Looking ahead, business sentiment moderated and remained below the long-run survey average since 2017.
